block_03: The highlight of last week was the U.S. Federal Reserve meeting — an important date on any trader's calendar for one big reason: interest rates. This time, stocks fell, and the dollar index jumped 0.41% after the FED pulled interest rate hikes into 2023, sooner than previously projected.
block_06: $4 billion loss after Cristiano Ronaldo's gesture of putting aside two bottles of Coca-Cola at a press conference. In fact, Coke shares were already down 0.9% before Ronaldo even took his seat for the press conference. Possible explanation: Ronaldo's eccentric gesture coincided with Coca-Cola's ex-dividend date when the company shared its profit with the shareholders 🤷🏻‍♂️

block_45: Do you know that the first book on trading was written 333 years ago?! According to "Confusion of Confusions" by José Peso de la Vega, even back in 1688, people traded futures and options and participated in the "bulls" and "bears" conspiracies. Things don't seem to change much in three centuries, huh?
